title: SSO Setup Issues — Troubleshooting SAML Configuration
content:
Single Sign-On (SSO) for CloudDash uses SAML 2.0 and is available on Pro (with add-on) and Enterprise plans. Setup issues typically involve metadata mismatches, attribute mapping errors, or certificate expiration.

Initial setup requires configuring CloudDash as a SAML service provider in your identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, or OneLogin). Download CloudDash SP metadata from Settings > Security > SSO > SAML Configuration. Import this metadata into your IdP, or manually configure: ACS URL (https://app.clouddash.io/auth/saml/callback), Entity ID (https://app.clouddash.io), and NameID format (emailAddress).

Required SAML attributes: CloudDash expects NameID or email attribute for user identification, and optionally firstName, lastName, and groups for role mapping. In Okta, map groups to CloudDash roles under Attribute Statements: groups -> memberOf. In Azure AD, configure group claims in Token Configuration.

Common error: 'SAML Response Invalid Signature' — the IdP signing certificate expired or was rotated without updating CloudDash. Upload the new IdP certificate under Settings > Security > SSO > Identity Provider Certificate. CloudDash supports multiple certificates during rotation periods.

Error: 'User Provisioning Failed' — the SAML response lacks a valid email attribute. Verify attribute mapping sends email in NameID or a custom attribute named 'email'. Test using your IdP's SAML tracer tool and compare the assertion against CloudDash requirements.

Just-in-time (JIT) provisioning creates CloudDash accounts on first SSO login. If JIT is disabled, users must be pre-invited via Team Management before SSO login succeeds. Enable JIT under SSO Settings for automatic account creation with default Viewer role.

After configuration changes, use the SSO Test Login button to validate without affecting production users. Enterprise customers can enable SSO enforcement to disable password-based login entirely.
